name: Code Runner
description: Run C, C++, Java, JS, PHP, Python, Perl, Ruby, Go, Lua, Groovy, PowerShell, CMD, BASH, F#, C#, VBScript, TypeScript, CoffeeScript, Scala, Swift, Julia, Crystal, OCaml, R, AppleScript, Elixir, VB.NET, Clojure, Haxe, Obj-C, Rust, Racket, Scheme, AutoHotkey, AutoIt, Kotlin, Dart, Pascal, Haskell, Nim, D, Lisp, Kit, V, SCSS, Sass, CUDA, Less, Fortran, Ring, Standard ML, Zig, Mojo, Erlang, SPWN, Pkl, Gleam

store_metadata.name: Code Runner
store_metadata.short_description: Run C, C++, Java, JS, PHP, Python, Perl, Ruby, Go, Lua, Groovy, PowerShell, CMD, BASH, F#, C#, VBScript, TypeScript, CoffeeScript, Scala, Swift, Julia, Crystal, OCaml, R, AppleScript, Elixir, VB.NET, Clojure, Haxe, Obj-C, Rust, Racket, Scheme, AutoHotkey, AutoIt, Kotlin, Dart, Pascal, Haskell, Nim,

findings.detail: Activation event 'onCommand:code-runner.run': Runs when command triggered - user/shortcut driven
Activation event 'onCommand:code-runner.runCustomCommand': Runs when command triggered - user/shortcut driven
Activation event 'onCommand:code-runner.runByLanguage': Runs when command triggered - user/shortcut driven
Activation event 'onStartupFinished': Runs every launch - immediate execution

formulahendry.code-runner is affected by CVE-2025-65715: Code Runner vulnerability: executor commands from settings.json are passed directly into child_process.spawn(..., { shell: true }) allowing arbitrary code execution when settings are manipulated.

findings.name: Publisher-domain mismatch: formulahendry vs www.patreon.com
findings.description: Publisher "formulahendry" does not match operational domain "www.patreon.com"

findings.detail: micromatch@4.0.2 has known vulnerability GHSA-952p-6rrq-rcjv — Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) in micromatch (fix: >=4.0.8)

vulns.summary: Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) in micromatch

all_findings.name: Shell execution with user-controlled input (same file)
all_findings.description: User input source (QuickPick/InputBox) and shell command execution exist in the same file - user input may reach shell commands. Review whether input is properly sanitized.
all_findings.evidence: User input + shell execution co-located in: out\src\codeManager.js

all_findings.name: Local tool integration: workspace monitoring + command execution (no network)
all_findings.description: Extension monitors workspace activity and executes commands but has NO network access. Consistent with local CLI tool integration. High capability but justified pattern.
all_findings.evidence: Document fileName/path collection + child_process.exec with shell=true + no network

medium_risk.description: Monitors text document changes in real time

low_risk.description: Tracks developer activity (file opens, saves, folder changes, paths)

signals.description: Extension has no network capabilities - data stays local
No code obfuscation or evasion techniques detected
No access to credential files, SSH keys, or token stores
Extension activates only on specific events, not wildcard

risk_classification.summary: High-risk capabilities detected but strong positive signals indicate legitimate purpose. No obfuscation, no credential theft, behavior matches expected extension functionality. Capability is justified but users should be aware of the access granted.
